title: palala malabarica
intro: Palala malabarica, also known as Malabar Nut, is a deciduous tree native to the tropical forests of India and Sri Lanka, belonging to the family Simaroubaceae. The medicinal properties of Palala malabarica lie in its bark, which contains a compound called nimbin, a natural anti-inflammatory and antipyretic. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, while also being used as an anti-diarrheal and anti-flatulent. The herbal preparations of Palala malabarica include an infusion made from its bark, which is often consumed in the form of a decoction or a powder, and is also used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various ailments.
